g.text: Arista Networks (ANET) — 63% gross margin business growing revenue 22.8% with
• Gross margin of 63.4% suggests durable pricing power.
• Revenue growth at +22.8% above broad-market average.
• Leverage at 0.00x net debt/EBITDA leaves balance-sheet headroom.
• FCF yield of 3.1% may be thin if growth slows.
• Market cap of $96.4B exposes investors to multiple compression.
• Quality multiples typically compress in higher-rate regimes.
• Customer or end-market concentration could reverse the growth trend.
• Macro / rate sensitivity in the valuation multiple.
• Watch for capex acceleration or working-capital deterioration.
Stock-based compensation as a meaningful share of revenue
Insider selling or recent management departures
Aggressive non-GAAP accounting or earnings adjustments
